name.description: The name of the artifact to add.

artifact.description: The id of the artifact to add, exactly as returned by the tool that created it. Do NOT guess or generate the id.

tools.name: create

doc.description:
The URI of the referenced object. Protocols accepted:
- echo://01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4. 
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)
- dxn:org.dxos.type.calendar:0.1.0 -- named entity from the registry. Absoutely make sure it actually exists in the registry.

URIs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.

## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You work on an agent. Each agent has instructions - the goal of the agent.
  The agent plan shows the current progress of the agent.
  Agent has a number of associated artifacts you can read/write.
  You can edit them if necessary.
  
  IMPORTANT: When creating a new artifact, always add it to the agent using the add-artifact function.
  
  <agent id="01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNT95" name="Test Agent">
    <instructions>
      A test agent for adding artifacts.
    </instructions>
    <plan>
      No plan found.
    </plan>
  
    <artifacts>
    </artifacts>
  </agent>
</skill>

<skill>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</skill>

response.delta:
've created a shopping list for scrambled eggs on toast. Here are the ingredients:

- **Eggs** – the star

of the dish
- **Bread** – for the toast
- **Butter** – for cooking the eggs and spreading on toast
- **Milk** – for cre

amier scrambled eggs
- **Salt** – to season
- **Black pepper** – to season
- **Cooking oil** – optional, as an alternative to butter
Would you like me to add anything else, such as cheese, chives, or a side like bacon or tomatoes?

content.content:
## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You work on an agent. Each agent has instructions - the goal of the agent.
  The agent plan shows the current progress of the agent.
  Agent has a number of associated artifacts you can read/write.
  You can edit them if necessary.
  
  IMPORTANT: When creating a new artifact, always add it to the agent using the add-artifact function.
  
  <agent id="01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNTH4" name="Expense tracking">
    <instructions>
      Keep a list of expenses in a markdown document (create artifact "Expenses").
  Process incoming emails, add the relevant ones to the list.
  
  Format:
  
  ## Expenses
  - Flight to London (2026-02-01): £100
  - Hotel in London (2026-02-01): £100
    </instructions>
    <plan>
      No plan found.
    </plan>
  
    <artifacts>
      <artifact type="org.dxos.type.document" dxn="echo://BDJXMULPW5FB55UZ7KP5HLDHVTXUTHE2U/01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNTKJ">
        Expenses
      </artifact>
    </artifacts>
  </agent>
</skill>

<skill>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</skill>

## Context Objects

<object>
  <dxn>echo://BDJXMULPW5FB55UZ7KP5HLDHVTXUTHE2U/01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNTH4</dxn>

content.content:
## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You work on an agent. Each agent has instructions - the goal of the agent.
  The agent plan shows the current progress of the agent.
  Agent has a number of associated artifacts you can read/write.
  You can edit them if necessary.
  
  IMPORTANT: When creating a new artifact, always add it to the agent using the add-artifact function.
  
  <agent id="01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNTH4" name="Expense tracking">
    <instructions>
      Keep a list of expenses in a markdown document (create artifact "Expenses").
  Process incoming emails, add the relevant ones to the list.
  
  Format:
  
  ## Expenses
  - Flight to London (2026-02-01): £100
  - Hotel in London (2026-02-01): £100
    </instructions>
    <plan>
      No plan found.
    </plan>
  
    <artifacts>
      <artifact type="org.dxos.type.document" dxn="echo://BDJXMULPW5FB55UZ7KP5HLDHVTXUTHE2U/01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNTKJ">
        Expenses
      </artifact>
    </artifacts>
  </agent>
</skill>

<skill>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</skill>

## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You work on an agent. Each agent has instructions - the goal of the agent.
  The agent plan shows the current progress of the agent.
  Agent has a number of associated artifacts you can read/write.
  You can edit them if necessary.
  
  IMPORTANT: When creating a new artifact, always add it to the agent using the add-artifact function.
  
  <agent id="01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNTH4" name="Expense tracking">
    <instructions>
      Keep a list of expenses in a markdown document (create artifact "Expenses").
  Process incoming emails, add the relevant ones to the list.
  
  Format:
  
  ## Expenses
  - Flight to London (2026-02-01): £100
  - Hotel in London (2026-02-01): £100
    </instructions>
    <plan>
      No plan found.
    </plan>
  
    <artifacts>
      <artifact type="org.dxos.type.document" dxn="echo://BDJXMULPW5FB55UZ7KP5HLDHVTXUTHE2U/01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNTKJ">
        Expenses
      </artifact>
    </artifacts>
  </agent>
</skill>

<skill>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</skill>

content.content:
## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You work on an agent. Each agent has instructions - the goal of the agent.
  The agent plan shows the current progress of the agent.
  Agent has a number of associated artifacts you can read/write.
  You can edit them if necessary.
  
  IMPORTANT: When creating a new artifact, always add it to the agent using the add-artifact function.
  
  <agent id="01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNTH4" name="Expense tracking">
    <instructions>
      Keep a list of expenses in a markdown document (create artifact "Expenses").
  Process incoming emails, add the relevant ones to the list.
  
  Format:
  
  ## Expenses
  - Flight to London (2026-02-01): £100
  - Hotel in London (2026-02-01): £100
    </instructions>
    <plan>
      No plan found.
    </plan>
  
    <artifacts>
      <artifact type="org.dxos.type.document" dxn="echo://BDJXMULPW5FB55UZ7KP5HLDHVTXUTHE2U/01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNTKJ">
        Expenses
      </artifact>
    </artifacts>
  </agent>
</skill>

<skill>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</skill>

## Context Objects

<object>
  <dxn>echo://BDJXMULPW5FB55UZ7KP5HLDHVTXUTHE2U/01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNTH4</dxn>
  <typename>org.dxos.type.agent</typename>
</object>

<object>
  <dxn>echo://BDJXMULPW5FB55UZ7KP5HLDHVTXUTHE2U/01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNTHF</dxn>
  <typename>org.dxos.type.assistant.chat</typename>
</object>

tools.name: add-artifact
tools.description: Adds a new artifact.

name.description: The name of the artifact to add.

artifact.description: The id of the artifact to add, exactly as returned by the tool that created it. Do NOT guess or generate the id.

tools.name: create
tools.description: Creates a new markdown document and adds it to the space.

tools.name: open
tools.description: Opens and reads the contents of a new markdown document.

doc.description:
The URI of the referenced object. Protocols accepted:
- echo://01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4. 
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)
- dxn:org.dxos.type.calendar:0.1.0 -- named entity from the registry. Absoutely make sure it actually exists in the registry.

URIs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.

tools.name: update
tools.description: Applies a set of edits to the markdown document.

doc.description:
The URI of the referenced object. Protocols accepted:
- echo://01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4. 
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)
- dxn:org.dxos.type.calendar:0.1.0 -- named entity from the registry. Absoutely make sure it actually exists in the registry.

URIs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.

oldString.description: The text to find in the document. Set to undefined to append the newString to the end of the document.

newString.description: The text to replace it with.

replaceAll.description: If true, replaces all occurrences. Defaults to false (first occurrence only).

edits.description: The edits to apply to the document. Each edit finds oldString and replaces it with newString; omit oldString to append newString to the end.

## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You work on an agent. Each agent has instructions - the goal of the agent.
  The agent plan shows the current progress of the agent.
  Agent has a number of associated artifacts you can read/write.
  You can edit them if necessary.
  
  IMPORTANT: When creating a new artifact, always add it to the agent using the add-artifact function.
  
  <agent id="01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNTH4" name="Expense tracking">
    <instructions>
      Keep a list of expenses in a markdown document (create artifact "Expenses").
  Process incoming emails, add the relevant ones to the list.
  
  Format:
  
  ## Expenses
  - Flight to London (2026-02-01): £100
  - Hotel in London (2026-02-01): £100
    </instructions>
    <plan>
      No plan found.
    </plan>
  
    <artifacts>

      </artifact>
    </artifacts>
  </agent>
</skill>

<skill>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:

  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."

  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."

tools.name: add-artifact
tools.description: Adds a new artifact.

name.description: The name of the artifact to add.

artifact.description: The id of the artifact to add, exactly as returned by the tool that created it. Do NOT guess or generate the id.

tools.name: create
tools.description: Creates a new markdown document and adds it to the space.

tools.name: open
tools.description: Opens and reads the contents of a new markdown document.

doc.description:
The URI of the referenced object. Protocols accepted:
- echo://01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4. 
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)
- dxn:org.dxos.type.calendar:0.1.0 -- named entity from the registry. Absoutely make sure it actually exists in the registry.

URIs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.

tools.name: update
tools.description: Applies a set of edits to the markdown document.

doc.description:
The URI of the referenced object. Protocols accepted:
- echo://01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4. 
- space ID, object ID tuple (spaceID:objectID)
- Only object ID that is assumed to be in the current space (01KG7R1ZXWFMWQ4DA1Q6TN1DG4)
- dxn:org.dxos.type.calendar:0.1.0 -- named entity from the registry. Absoutely make sure it actually exists in the registry.

URIs can be prepended with an @ symbol for compatibility with in-text references.

oldString.description: The text to find in the document. Set to undefined to append the newString to the end of the document.

newString.description: The text to replace it with.

replaceAll.description: If true, replaces all occurrences. Defaults to false (first occurrence only).

edits.description: The edits to apply to the document. Each edit finds oldString and replaces it with newString; omit oldString to append newString to the end.

content.content:
## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You work on an agent. Each agent has instructions - the goal of the agent.
  The agent plan shows the current progress of the agent.
  Agent has a number of associated artifacts you can read/write.
  You can edit them if necessary.
  
  IMPORTANT: When creating a new artifact, always add it to the agent using the add-artifact function.
  
  <agent id="01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNTH4" name="Expense tracking">
    <instructions>
      Keep a list of expenses in a markdown document (create artifact "Expenses").
  Process incoming emails, add the relevant ones to the list.
  
  Format:
  
  ## Expenses
  - Flight to London (2026-02-01): £100
  - Hotel in London (2026-02-01): £100
    </instructions>
    <plan>
      No plan found.
    </plan>
  
    <artifacts>
      <artifact type="org.dxos.type.document" dxn="echo://BDJXMULPW5FB55UZ7KP5HLDHVTXUTHE2U/01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNTKJ">
        Expenses
      </artifact>
    </artifacts>
  </agent>
</skill>

<skill>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</skill>

## Context Objects

<object>
  <dxn>echo://BDJXMULPW5FB55UZ7KP5HLDHVTXUTHE2U/01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNTH4</dxn>
  <typename>org.dxos.type.agent</typename>
</object>

<object>
  <dxn>echo://BDJXMULPW5FB55UZ7KP5HLDHVTXUTHE2U/01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNTHF</dxn>
  <typename>org.dxos.type.assistant.chat</typename>
</object>

tools.name: update-tasks
tools.description:
## PLANNING TOOL

This tool maintains an organized task list.
Use this to track progress, break down objectives, and ensure thoroughness.
If you are asked to create a plan use this tool instead of creating a new document.
After creating initial tasks, update them silently without announcing changes to the user.
Important: Do not show or summarize the contents of the task list unless the user asks for this.

### CORE USAGE PRINCIPLES

Create and manage tasks for: multi-step objectives requiring 3+ distinct actions, complex projects needing careful sequencing,
user requests for task organization, multiple deliverables provided together, new instructions (capture as tasks immediately with new IDs),
completed work (mark `done` and add follow-ups), and active work (mark as `in-progress`, limit one at a time).

Skip task management for: single straightforward actions, simple requests achievable in 1-2 steps,
informational queries, quick lookups or clarifications, and avoid creating verification tasks unless requested.

### TOOL SPECIFICATION

`update-tasks` requires an array of task objects. Each task object contains:

- id (string, required): unique identifier like "task_1" or "research_sources"
- title (string, optional): update to clarify or refine task description
- status (string, optional): `todo` | `in-progress` | `done`

Task status meanings: 'todo' means not yet started, `in-progress` means currently being worked on, `done` means completed successfully.

### OPERATIONAL GUIDELINES

Update tasks in realtime as work progresses. Mark tasks `done` immediately upon completion.
Maintain only ONE task with `in-progress` status at a time. Complete current tasks before starting new ones.
Use specific, actionable task titles. Break complex work into manageable logical pieces.
Batch task updates with other actions when possible for efficiency.

### USAGE EXAMPLES

<example type="research_project">
<user_message>I need to research sustainable packaging options and write a report comparing costs and environmental impact.</user_message>
<assistant_action>
Creates tasks:
1. {id: "research_materials", title: "Research sustainable packaging materials", status: "in-progress"}
2. {id: "compare_costs", title: "Compile cost comparison data", status: "todo"}
3. {id: "assess_impact", title: "Evaluate environmental impact metrics", status: "todo"}
4. {id: "draft_report", title: "Write comparative analysis report", status: "todo"}

Begins research work immediately in same response.
</assistant_action>
<reasoning>Multi-phase project requiring systematic tracking across research, analysis, and writing stages.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="event_planning">
<user_message>Help me plan a team retreat - venue, agenda, catering, and send invitations.</user_message>
<assistant_action>
Creates tasks:
1. {id: "venue_options", title: "Research and recommend venues", status: "in-progress"}
2. {id: "draft_agenda", title: "Create retreat agenda", status: "todo"}
3. {id: "arrange_catering", title: "Coordinate catering options", status: "todo"}
4. {id: "send_invites", title: "Draft and send invitations", status: "todo"}
</assistant_action>
<reasoning>Multiple distinct deliverables with dependencies requiring organized tracking.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="document_analysis">
<user_message>Review this 30-page contract for key terms, risks, and negotiation points.</user_message>
<assistant_action>
Creates tasks:
1. {id: "extract_key_terms", title: "Identify key contractual terms", status: "in-progress"}
2. {id: "flag_risks", title: "Flag potential risks and liabilities", status: "todo"}
3. {id: "negotiation_points", title: "Compile negotiation recommendations", status: "todo"}
4. {id: "summary_doc", title: "Prepare executive summary", status: "todo"}
</assistant_action>
<reasoning>Substantial analysis work requiring methodical breakdown and systematic review.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="multiple_deliverables">
<user_message>I need: 1) competitor analysis for three companies, 2) SWOT analysis, 3) market positioning recommendations.</user_message>
<assistant_action>
Creates tasks:
1. {id: "competitor_research", title: "Research three competitor companies", status: "in-progress"}
2. {id: "swot_analysis", title: "Develop SWOT analysis", status: "todo"}
3. {id: "positioning_recs", title: "Create market positioning recommendations", status: "todo"}
</assistant_action>
<reasoning>User provided numbered list of distinct deliverables requiring separate effort.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="skip_simple_question">
<user_message>What's the difference between renewable and sustainable energy?</user_message>
<assistant_action>Provides explanation directly without creating tasks.</assistant_action>
<reasoning>Informational request with no actionable work to complete or track.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="skip_quick_lookup">
<user_message>Find the population of Tokyo.</user_message>
<assistant_action>Searches and provides answer without task tracking.</assistant_action>
<reasoning>Single straightforward lookup completable immediately.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="skip_trivial_task">
<user_message>Summarize this 2-paragraph email.</user_message>
<assistant_action>Provides summary directly without creating tasks.</assistant_action>
<reasoning>Single simple action requiring no breakdown or progress tracking.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="skip_single_action">
<user_message>Check if my flight is on time.</user_message>
<assistant_action>Performs lookup and reports status without task management.</assistant_action>
<reasoning>One-step action with immediate completion, no organizational benefit from tasks.</reasoning>
</example>

### BEST PRACTICES

For task creation: use descriptive unique IDs reflecting the work, start first task as `in-progress`, batch initial creation with beginning work.
For progress tracking: update status immediately upon completion, keep only one `in-progress` task unless parallel work is natural, add follow-up tasks as they emerge. For task breakdown: aim for reasonably-scoped tasks, group related small actions into logical units, split tasks requiring different approaches.

When uncertain whether to use task management, err on the side of creating tasks.
Proactive organization demonstrates thoroughness and ensures comprehensive work completion.

content.content:
You are a helpful assistant that can help with tasks in the outside world.
Be transparent about what you are doing and what you are not doing.
If you do not have tools to complete the task, inform the user.
DO NOT PRETEND TO DO SOMETHING YOU CAN'T DO.

## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You work on an agent. Each agent has instructions - the goal of the agent.
  The agent plan shows the current progress of the agent.
  Agent has a number of associated artifacts you can read/write.
  You can edit them if necessary.
  
  IMPORTANT: When creating a new artifact, always add it to the agent using the add-artifact function.
  
  <agent id="01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNV5C" name="Egg making">
    <instructions>
      I'm testing how planning (task management) works.
  Create tasks to make scrambled eggs.
  
  Then simulate this plan execution in a markdown document.
  The document should reflect the state of all objects involved in the cooking process.
  The document should also have the log of actions taken.
  
  Important: simualte actions one by one, in the order they are listed.
  Simlate by updating the local document.
  
  <example>
    # State
  
    - 2 raw eggs
    - 1 frying pan
    
    # Action log
    
    - Taken 2 raw eggs out of the fridge.
  </example>
    </instructions>
    <plan>
      No plan found.
    </plan>
  
    <artifacts>
    </artifacts>
  </agent>
</skill>

<skill>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</skill>

<skill>
  
</skill>

## Context Objects

<object>
  <dxn>echo://BDJXMULPW5FB55UZ7KP5HLDHVTXUTHE2U/01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNV5C</dxn>
  <typename>org.dxos.type.agent</typename>
</object>

<object>
  <dxn>echo://BDJXMULPW5FB55UZ7KP5HLDHVTXUTHE2U/01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNV5T</dxn>
  <typename>org.dxos.type.assistant.chat</typename>
</object>
Go

# State

- 2 raw eggs (in fridge)
- 1 stick of butter (in fridge)
- 1 pinch of salt (in shaker)
- 1 frying pan (cold, on stove, empty)
- 1 mixing bowl (empty)
- 1 fork (clean)
- 1 spatula (clean)
- 1 plate (empty)
- stove (off)

# Action log

- (none yet)

tools.name: update-tasks
tools.description:
## PLANNING TOOL

This tool maintains an organized task list.
Use this to track progress, break down objectives, and ensure thoroughness.
If you are asked to create a plan use this tool instead of creating a new document.
After creating initial tasks, update them silently without announcing changes to the user.
Important: Do not show or summarize the contents of the task list unless the user asks for this.

### CORE USAGE PRINCIPLES

Create and manage tasks for: multi-step objectives requiring 3+ distinct actions, complex projects needing careful sequencing,
user requests for task organization, multiple deliverables provided together, new instructions (capture as tasks immediately with new IDs),
completed work (mark `done` and add follow-ups), and active work (mark as `in-progress`, limit one at a time).

Skip task management for: single straightforward actions, simple requests achievable in 1-2 steps,
informational queries, quick lookups or clarifications, and avoid creating verification tasks unless requested.

### TOOL SPECIFICATION

`update-tasks` requires an array of task objects. Each task object contains:

- id (string, required): unique identifier like "task_1" or "research_sources"
- title (string, optional): update to clarify or refine task description
- status (string, optional): `todo` | `in-progress` | `done`

Task status meanings: 'todo' means not yet started, `in-progress` means currently being worked on, `done` means completed successfully.

### OPERATIONAL GUIDELINES

Update tasks in realtime as work progresses. Mark tasks `done` immediately upon completion.
Maintain only ONE task with `in-progress` status at a time. Complete current tasks before starting new ones.
Use specific, actionable task titles. Break complex work into manageable logical pieces.
Batch task updates with other actions when possible for efficiency.

### USAGE EXAMPLES

<example type="research_project">
<user_message>I need to research sustainable packaging options and write a report comparing costs and environmental impact.</user_message>
<assistant_action>
Creates tasks:
1. {id: "research_materials", title: "Research sustainable packaging materials", status: "in-progress"}
2. {id: "compare_costs", title: "Compile cost comparison data", status: "todo"}
3. {id: "assess_impact", title: "Evaluate environmental impact metrics", status: "todo"}
4. {id: "draft_report", title: "Write comparative analysis report", status: "todo"}

Begins research work immediately in same response.
</assistant_action>
<reasoning>Multi-phase project requiring systematic tracking across research, analysis, and writing stages.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="event_planning">
<user_message>Help me plan a team retreat - venue, agenda, catering, and send invitations.</user_message>
<assistant_action>
Creates tasks:
1. {id: "venue_options", title: "Research and recommend venues", status: "in-progress"}
2. {id: "draft_agenda", title: "Create retreat agenda", status: "todo"}
3. {id: "arrange_catering", title: "Coordinate catering options", status: "todo"}
4. {id: "send_invites", title: "Draft and send invitations", status: "todo"}
</assistant_action>
<reasoning>Multiple distinct deliverables with dependencies requiring organized tracking.</reasoning>
</example>

3. {id: "negotiation_points", title: "Compile negotiation recommendations", status: "todo"}

2. {id: "swot_analysis", title: "Develop SWOT analysis", status: "todo"}

</assistant_action>

tools.name: update-tasks
tools.description:
## PLANNING TOOL

This tool maintains an organized task list.
Use this to track progress, break down objectives, and ensure thoroughness.
If you are asked to create a plan use this tool instead of creating a new document.
After creating initial tasks, update them silently without announcing changes to the user.
Important: Do not show or summarize the contents of the task list unless the user asks for this.

### CORE USAGE PRINCIPLES

Create and manage tasks for: multi-step objectives requiring 3+ distinct actions, complex projects needing careful sequencing,
user requests for task organization, multiple deliverables provided together, new instructions (capture as tasks immediately with new IDs),
completed work (mark `done` and add follow-ups), and active work (mark as `in-progress`, limit one at a time).

Skip task management for: single straightforward actions, simple requests achievable in 1-2 steps,
informational queries, quick lookups or clarifications, and avoid creating verification tasks unless requested.

### TOOL SPECIFICATION

`update-tasks` requires an array of task objects. Each task object contains:

- id (string, required): unique identifier like "task_1" or "research_sources"
- title (string, optional): update to clarify or refine task description
- status (string, optional): `todo` | `in-progress` | `done`

Task status meanings: 'todo' means not yet started, `in-progress` means currently being worked on, `done` means completed successfully.

### OPERATIONAL GUIDELINES

Update tasks in realtime as work progresses. Mark tasks `done` immediately upon completion.
Maintain only ONE task with `in-progress` status at a time. Complete current tasks before starting new ones.
Use specific, actionable task titles. Break complex work into manageable logical pieces.
Batch task updates with other actions when possible for efficiency.

### USAGE EXAMPLES

<example type="research_project">
<user_message>I need to research sustainable packaging options and write a report comparing costs and environmental impact.</user_message>
<assistant_action>
Creates tasks:
1. {id: "research_materials", title: "Research sustainable packaging materials", status: "in-progress"}
2. {id: "compare_costs", title: "Compile cost comparison data", status: "todo"}
3. {id: "assess_impact", title: "Evaluate environmental impact metrics", status: "todo"}
4. {id: "draft_report", title: "Write comparative analysis report", status: "todo"}

Begins research work immediately in same response.
</assistant_action>
<reasoning>Multi-phase project requiring systematic tracking across research, analysis, and writing stages.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="event_planning">
<user_message>Help me plan a team retreat - venue, agenda, catering, and send invitations.</user_message>
<assistant_action>
Creates tasks:
1. {id: "venue_options", title: "Research and recommend venues", status: "in-progress"}
2. {id: "draft_agenda", title: "Create retreat agenda", status: "todo"}
3. {id: "arrange_catering", title: "Coordinate catering options", status: "todo"}
4. {id: "send_invites", title: "Draft and send invitations", status: "todo"}
</assistant_action>
<reasoning>Multiple distinct deliverables with dependencies requiring organized tracking.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="document_analysis">
<user_message>Review this 30-page contract for key terms, risks, and negotiation points.</user_message>
<assistant_action>
Creates tasks:
1. {id: "extract_key_terms", title: "Identify key contractual terms", status: "in-progress"}
2. {id: "flag_risks", title: "Flag potential risks and liabilities", status: "todo"}
3. {id: "negotiation_points", title: "Compile negotiation recommendations", status: "todo"}
4. {id: "summary_doc", title: "Prepare executive summary", status: "todo"}
</assistant_action>
<reasoning>Substantial analysis work requiring methodical breakdown and systematic review.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="multiple_deliverables">
<user_message>I need: 1) competitor analysis for three companies, 2) SWOT analysis, 3) market positioning recommendations.</user_message>
<assistant_action>
Creates tasks:
1. {id: "competitor_research", title: "Research three competitor companies", status: "in-progress"}
2. {id: "swot_analysis", title: "Develop SWOT analysis", status: "todo"}
3. {id: "positioning_recs", title: "Create market positioning recommendations", status: "todo"}
</assistant_action>
<reasoning>User provided numbered list of distinct deliverables requiring separate effort.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="skip_simple_question">
<user_message>What's the difference between renewable and sustainable energy?</user_message>
<assistant_action>Provides explanation directly without creating tasks.</assistant_action>
<reasoning>Informational request with no actionable work to complete or track.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="skip_quick_lookup">
<user_message>Find the population of Tokyo.</user_message>
<assistant_action>Searches and provides answer without task tracking.</assistant_action>
<reasoning>Single straightforward lookup completable immediately.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="skip_trivial_task">
<user_message>Summarize this 2-paragraph email.</user_message>
<assistant_action>Provides summary directly without creating tasks.</assistant_action>
<reasoning>Single simple action requiring no breakdown or progress tracking.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="skip_single_action">
<user_message>Check if my flight is on time.</user_message>
<assistant_action>Performs lookup and reports status without task management.</assistant_action>
<reasoning>One-step action with immediate completion, no organizational benefit from tasks.</reasoning>
</example>

### BEST PRACTICES

For task creation: use descriptive unique IDs reflecting the work, start first task as `in-progress`, batch initial creation with beginning work.
For progress tracking: update status immediately upon completion, keep only one `in-progress` task unless parallel work is natural, add follow-up tasks as they emerge. For task breakdown: aim for reasonably-scoped tasks, group related small actions into logical units, split tasks requiring different approaches.

When uncertain whether to use task management, err on the side of creating tasks.
Proactive organization demonstrates thoroughness and ensures comprehensive work completion.

tools.name: update-tasks
tools.description:
## PLANNING TOOL

This tool maintains an organized task list.
Use this to track progress, break down objectives, and ensure thoroughness.
If you are asked to create a plan use this tool instead of creating a new document.
After creating initial tasks, update them silently without announcing changes to the user.
Important: Do not show or summarize the contents of the task list unless the user asks for this.

### CORE USAGE PRINCIPLES

Create and manage tasks for: multi-step objectives requiring 3+ distinct actions, complex projects needing careful sequencing,
user requests for task organization, multiple deliverables provided together, new instructions (capture as tasks immediately with new IDs),
completed work (mark `done` and add follow-ups), and active work (mark as `in-progress`, limit one at a time).

Skip task management for: single straightforward actions, simple requests achievable in 1-2 steps,
informational queries, quick lookups or clarifications, and avoid creating verification tasks unless requested.

### TOOL SPECIFICATION

`update-tasks` requires an array of task objects. Each task object contains:

- id (string, required): unique identifier like "task_1" or "research_sources"
- title (string, optional): update to clarify or refine task description
- status (string, optional): `todo` | `in-progress` | `done`

Task status meanings: 'todo' means not yet started, `in-progress` means currently being worked on, `done` means completed successfully.

### OPERATIONAL GUIDELINES

Update tasks in realtime as work progresses. Mark tasks `done` immediately upon completion.
Maintain only ONE task with `in-progress` status at a time. Complete current tasks before starting new ones.
Use specific, actionable task titles. Break complex work into manageable logical pieces.
Batch task updates with other actions when possible for efficiency.

### USAGE EXAMPLES

<example type="research_project">
<user_message>I need to research sustainable packaging options and write a report comparing costs and environmental impact.</user_message>
<assistant_action>
Creates tasks:
1. {id: "research_materials", title: "Research sustainable packaging materials", status: "in-progress"}
2. {id: "compare_costs", title: "Compile cost comparison data", status: "todo"}
3. {id: "assess_impact", title: "Evaluate environmental impact metrics", status: "todo"}
4. {id: "draft_report", title: "Write comparative analysis report", status: "todo"}

Begins research work immediately in same response.
</assistant_action>
<reasoning>Multi-phase project requiring systematic tracking across research, analysis, and writing stages.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="event_planning">
<user_message>Help me plan a team retreat - venue, agenda, catering, and send invitations.</user_message>
<assistant_action>
Creates tasks:
1. {id: "venue_options", title: "Research and recommend venues", status: "in-progress"}
2. {id: "draft_agenda", title: "Create retreat agenda", status: "todo"}
3. {id: "arrange_catering", title: "Coordinate catering options", status: "todo"}
4. {id: "send_invites", title: "Draft and send invitations", status: "todo"}
</assistant_action>
<reasoning>Multiple distinct deliverables with dependencies requiring organized tracking.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="document_analysis">
<user_message>Review this 30-page contract for key terms, risks, and negotiation points.</user_message>
<assistant_action>
Creates tasks:
1. {id: "extract_key_terms", title: "Identify key contractual terms", status: "in-progress"}
2. {id: "flag_risks", title: "Flag potential risks and liabilities", status: "todo"}
3. {id: "negotiation_points", title: "Compile negotiation recommendations", status: "todo"}
4. {id: "summary_doc", title: "Prepare executive summary", status: "todo"}
</assistant_action>
<reasoning>Substantial analysis work requiring methodical breakdown and systematic review.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="multiple_deliverables">
<user_message>I need: 1) competitor analysis for three companies, 2) SWOT analysis, 3) market positioning recommendations.</user_message>
<assistant_action>
Creates tasks:
1. {id: "competitor_research", title: "Research three competitor companies", status: "in-progress"}
2. {id: "swot_analysis", title: "Develop SWOT analysis", status: "todo"}
3. {id: "positioning_recs", title: "Create market positioning recommendations", status: "todo"}
</assistant_action>
<reasoning>User provided numbered list of distinct deliverables requiring separate effort.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="skip_simple_question">
<user_message>What's the difference between renewable and sustainable energy?</user_message>
<assistant_action>Provides explanation directly without creating tasks.</assistant_action>
<reasoning>Informational request with no actionable work to complete or track.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="skip_quick_lookup">
<user_message>Find the population of Tokyo.</user_message>
<assistant_action>Searches and provides answer without task tracking.</assistant_action>
<reasoning>Single straightforward lookup completable immediately.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="skip_trivial_task">
<user_message>Summarize this 2-paragraph email.</user_message>
<assistant_action>Provides summary directly without creating tasks.</assistant_action>
<reasoning>Single simple action requiring no breakdown or progress tracking.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="skip_single_action">
<user_message>Check if my flight is on time.</user_message>
<assistant_action>Performs lookup and reports status without task management.</assistant_action>
<reasoning>One-step action with immediate completion, no organizational benefit from tasks.</reasoning>
</example>

### BEST PRACTICES

For task creation: use descriptive unique IDs reflecting the work, start first task as `in-progress`, batch initial creation with beginning work.
For progress tracking: update status immediately upon completion, keep only one `in-progress` task unless parallel work is natural, add follow-up tasks as they emerge. For task breakdown: aim for reasonably-scoped tasks, group related small actions into logical units, split tasks requiring different approaches.

When uncertain whether to use task management, err on the side of creating tasks.
Proactive organization demonstrates thoroughness and ensures comprehensive work completion.

content.content:
You are a helpful assistant that can help with tasks in the outside world.
Be transparent about what you are doing and what you are not doing.
If you do not have tools to complete the task, inform the user.
DO NOT PRETEND TO DO SOMETHING YOU CAN'T DO.

## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You work on an agent. Each agent has instructions - the goal of the agent.
  The agent plan shows the current progress of the agent.
  Agent has a number of associated artifacts you can read/write.
  You can edit them if necessary.
  
  IMPORTANT: When creating a new artifact, always add it to the agent using the add-artifact function.
  
  <agent id="01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNV5C" name="Egg making">
    <instructions>
      I'm testing how planning (task management) works.
  Create tasks to make scrambled eggs.
  
  Then simulate this plan execution in a markdown document.
  The document should reflect the state of all objects involved in the cooking process.
  The document should also have the log of actions taken.
  
  Important: simualte actions one by one, in the order they are listed.
  Simlate by updating the local document.
  
  <example>
    # State
  
    - 2 raw eggs
    - 1 frying pan
    
    # Action log
    
    - Taken 2 raw eggs out of the fridge.
  </example>
    </instructions>
    <plan>
      - **DONE**: Gather ingredients and tools (eggs, butter, salt, pan, bowl, fork, spatula) <!-- id=gather_ingredients -->
  - **DONE**: Crack eggs into a bowl <!-- id=crack_eggs -->
  - **DONE**: Beat eggs with salt <!-- id=beat_eggs -->
  - **DONE**: Heat pan and melt butter <!-- id=heat_pan -->
  - **IN-PROGRESS**: Pour beaten eggs into pan <!-- id=pour_eggs -->
  - **TODO**: Stir and scramble eggs until cooked <!-- id=scramble -->
  - **TODO**: Plate the scrambled eggs and serve <!-- id=plate -->
    </plan>
  
    <artifacts>
      <artifact type="org.dxos.type.document" dxn="echo://BDJXMULPW5FB55UZ7KP5HLDHVTXUTHE2U/01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNVBF">
        Scrambled Eggs Simulation
      </artifact>
    </artifacts>
  </agent>
</skill>

<skill>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</skill>

<skill>
  
</skill>

## Context Objects

<object>
  <dxn>echo://BDJXMULPW5FB55UZ7KP5HLDHVTXUTHE2U/01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNV5C</dxn>
  <typename>org.dxos.type.agent</typename>
</object>

</object>
Go

tools.name: update-tasks
tools.description:
## PLANNING TOOL

This tool maintains an organized task list.
Use this to track progress, break down objectives, and ensure thoroughness.
If you are asked to create a plan use this tool instead of creating a new document.
After creating initial tasks, update them silently without announcing changes to the user.
Important: Do not show or summarize the contents of the task list unless the user asks for this.

### CORE USAGE PRINCIPLES

Create and manage tasks for: multi-step objectives requiring 3+ distinct actions, complex projects needing careful sequencing,
user requests for task organization, multiple deliverables provided together, new instructions (capture as tasks immediately with new IDs),
completed work (mark `done` and add follow-ups), and active work (mark as `in-progress`, limit one at a time).

Skip task management for: single straightforward actions, simple requests achievable in 1-2 steps,
informational queries, quick lookups or clarifications, and avoid creating verification tasks unless requested.

### TOOL SPECIFICATION

`update-tasks` requires an array of task objects. Each task object contains:

- id (string, required): unique identifier like "task_1" or "research_sources"
- title (string, optional): update to clarify or refine task description
- status (string, optional): `todo` | `in-progress` | `done`

Task status meanings: 'todo' means not yet started, `in-progress` means currently being worked on, `done` means completed successfully.

### OPERATIONAL GUIDELINES

Update tasks in realtime as work progresses. Mark tasks `done` immediately upon completion.
Maintain only ONE task with `in-progress` status at a time. Complete current tasks before starting new ones.
Use specific, actionable task titles. Break complex work into manageable logical pieces.
Batch task updates with other actions when possible for efficiency.

### USAGE EXAMPLES

<example type="research_project">
<user_message>I need to research sustainable packaging options and write a report comparing costs and environmental impact.</user_message>
<assistant_action>
Creates tasks:
1. {id: "research_materials", title: "Research sustainable packaging materials", status: "in-progress"}
2. {id: "compare_costs", title: "Compile cost comparison data", status: "todo"}
3. {id: "assess_impact", title: "Evaluate environmental impact metrics", status: "todo"}
4. {id: "draft_report", title: "Write comparative analysis report", status: "todo"}

Begins research work immediately in same response.
</assistant_action>
<reasoning>Multi-phase project requiring systematic tracking across research, analysis, and writing stages.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="event_planning">
<user_message>Help me plan a team retreat - venue, agenda, catering, and send invitations.</user_message>
<assistant_action>
Creates tasks:
1. {id: "venue_options", title: "Research and recommend venues", status: "in-progress"}
2. {id: "draft_agenda", title: "Create retreat agenda", status: "todo"}
3. {id: "arrange_catering", title: "Coordinate catering options", status: "todo"}
4. {id: "send_invites", title: "Draft and send invitations", status: "todo"}
</assistant_action>
<reasoning>Multiple distinct deliverables with dependencies requiring organized tracking.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="document_analysis">
<user_message>Review this 30-page contract for key terms, risks, and negotiation points.</user_message>
<assistant_action>
Creates tasks:
1. {id: "extract_key_terms", title: "Identify key contractual terms", status: "in-progress"}
2. {id: "flag_risks", title: "Flag potential risks and liabilities", status: "todo"}
3. {id: "negotiation_points", title: "Compile negotiation recommendations", status: "todo"}
4. {id: "summary_doc", title: "Prepare executive summary", status: "todo"}
</assistant_action>
<reasoning>Substantial analysis work requiring methodical breakdown and systematic review.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="multiple_deliverables">
<user_message>I need: 1) competitor analysis for three companies, 2) SWOT analysis, 3) market positioning recommendations.</user_message>
<assistant_action>
Creates tasks:
1. {id: "competitor_research", title: "Research three competitor companies", status: "in-progress"}
2. {id: "swot_analysis", title: "Develop SWOT analysis", status: "todo"}
3. {id: "positioning_recs", title: "Create market positioning recommendations", status: "todo"}
</assistant_action>
<reasoning>User provided numbered list of distinct deliverables requiring separate effort.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="skip_simple_question">
<user_message>What's the difference between renewable and sustainable energy?</user_message>
<assistant_action>Provides explanation directly without creating tasks.</assistant_action>
<reasoning>Informational request with no actionable work to complete or track.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="skip_quick_lookup">
<user_message>Find the population of Tokyo.</user_message>
<assistant_action>Searches and provides answer without task tracking.</assistant_action>
<reasoning>Single straightforward lookup completable immediately.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="skip_trivial_task">
<user_message>Summarize this 2-paragraph email.</user_message>
<assistant_action>Provides summary directly without creating tasks.</assistant_action>
<reasoning>Single simple action requiring no breakdown or progress tracking.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="skip_single_action">
<user_message>Check if my flight is on time.</user_message>
<assistant_action>Performs lookup and reports status without task management.</assistant_action>
<reasoning>One-step action with immediate completion, no organizational benefit from tasks.</reasoning>
</example>

### BEST PRACTICES

For task creation: use descriptive unique IDs reflecting the work, start first task as `in-progress`, batch initial creation with beginning work.
For progress tracking: update status immediately upon completion, keep only one `in-progress` task unless parallel work is natural, add follow-up tasks as they emerge. For task breakdown: aim for reasonably-scoped tasks, group related small actions into logical units, split tasks requiring different approaches.

When uncertain whether to use task management, err on the side of creating tasks.
Proactive organization demonstrates thoroughness and ensures comprehensive work completion.

content.content:
You are a helpful assistant that can help with tasks in the outside world.
Be transparent about what you are doing and what you are not doing.
If you do not have tools to complete the task, inform the user.
DO NOT PRETEND TO DO SOMETHING YOU CAN'T DO.

## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You work on an agent. Each agent has instructions - the goal of the agent.
  The agent plan shows the current progress of the agent.
  Agent has a number of associated artifacts you can read/write.
  You can edit them if necessary.
  
  IMPORTANT: When creating a new artifact, always add it to the agent using the add-artifact function.
  
  <agent id="01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNV5C" name="Egg making">
    <instructions>
      I'm testing how planning (task management) works.
  Create tasks to make scrambled eggs.
  
  Then simulate this plan execution in a markdown document.
  The document should reflect the state of all objects involved in the cooking process.
  The document should also have the log of actions taken.
  
  Important: simualte actions one by one, in the order they are listed.
  Simlate by updating the local document.
  
  <example>
    # State
  
    - 2 raw eggs
    - 1 frying pan
    
    # Action log
    
    - Taken 2 raw eggs out of the fridge.
  </example>
    </instructions>
    <plan>
      - **DONE**: Gather ingredients and tools (eggs, butter, salt, pan, bowl, fork, spatula) <!-- id=gather_ingredients -->
  - **DONE**: Crack eggs into a bowl <!-- id=crack_eggs -->
  - **DONE**: Beat eggs with salt <!-- id=beat_eggs -->
  - **DONE**: Heat pan and melt butter <!-- id=heat_pan -->
  - **DONE**: Pour beaten eggs into pan <!-- id=pour_eggs -->
  - **IN-PROGRESS**: Stir and scramble eggs until cooked <!-- id=scramble -->
  - **TODO**: Plate the scrambled eggs and serve <!-- id=plate -->
    </plan>
  
    <artifacts>
      <artifact type="org.dxos.type.document" dxn="echo://BDJXMULPW5FB55UZ7KP5HLDHVTXUTHE2U/01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNVBF">
        Scrambled Eggs Simulation
      </artifact>
    </artifacts>
  </agent>
</skill>

<skill>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</skill>

<skill>
  
</skill>

## Context Objects

<object>
  <dxn>echo://BDJXMULPW5FB55UZ7KP5HLDHVTXUTHE2U/01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNV5C</dxn>
  <typename>org.dxos.type.agent</typename>
</object>

</object>
Go

- 0 pinches of salt (used)
- 1 salt shaker (on counter)

tools.name: update-tasks
tools.description:
## PLANNING TOOL

This tool maintains an organized task list.
Use this to track progress, break down objectives, and ensure thoroughness.
If you are asked to create a plan use this tool instead of creating a new document.
After creating initial tasks, update them silently without announcing changes to the user.
Important: Do not show or summarize the contents of the task list unless the user asks for this.

### CORE USAGE PRINCIPLES

Create and manage tasks for: multi-step objectives requiring 3+ distinct actions, complex projects needing careful sequencing,
user requests for task organization, multiple deliverables provided together, new instructions (capture as tasks immediately with new IDs),
completed work (mark `done` and add follow-ups), and active work (mark as `in-progress`, limit one at a time).

Skip task management for: single straightforward actions, simple requests achievable in 1-2 steps,
informational queries, quick lookups or clarifications, and avoid creating verification tasks unless requested.

### TOOL SPECIFICATION

`update-tasks` requires an array of task objects. Each task object contains:

- id (string, required): unique identifier like "task_1" or "research_sources"
- title (string, optional): update to clarify or refine task description
- status (string, optional): `todo` | `in-progress` | `done`

Task status meanings: 'todo' means not yet started, `in-progress` means currently being worked on, `done` means completed successfully.

### OPERATIONAL GUIDELINES

Update tasks in realtime as work progresses. Mark tasks `done` immediately upon completion.
Maintain only ONE task with `in-progress` status at a time. Complete current tasks before starting new ones.
Use specific, actionable task titles. Break complex work into manageable logical pieces.
Batch task updates with other actions when possible for efficiency.

### USAGE EXAMPLES

<example type="research_project">
<user_message>I need to research sustainable packaging options and write a report comparing costs and environmental impact.</user_message>
<assistant_action>
Creates tasks:
1. {id: "research_materials", title: "Research sustainable packaging materials", status: "in-progress"}
2. {id: "compare_costs", title: "Compile cost comparison data", status: "todo"}
3. {id: "assess_impact", title: "Evaluate environmental impact metrics", status: "todo"}
4. {id: "draft_report", title: "Write comparative analysis report", status: "todo"}

Begins research work immediately in same response.
</assistant_action>
<reasoning>Multi-phase project requiring systematic tracking across research, analysis, and writing stages.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="event_planning">
<user_message>Help me plan a team retreat - venue, agenda, catering, and send invitations.</user_message>
<assistant_action>
Creates tasks:
1. {id: "venue_options", title: "Research and recommend venues", status: "in-progress"}
2. {id: "draft_agenda", title: "Create retreat agenda", status: "todo"}
3. {id: "arrange_catering", title: "Coordinate catering options", status: "todo"}
4. {id: "send_invites", title: "Draft and send invitations", status: "todo"}
</assistant_action>
<reasoning>Multiple distinct deliverables with dependencies requiring organized tracking.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="document_analysis">
<user_message>Review this 30-page contract for key terms, risks, and negotiation points.</user_message>
<assistant_action>
Creates tasks:
1. {id: "extract_key_terms", title: "Identify key contractual terms", status: "in-progress"}
2. {id: "flag_risks", title: "Flag potential risks and liabilities", status: "todo"}
3. {id: "negotiation_points", title: "Compile negotiation recommendations", status: "todo"}
4. {id: "summary_doc", title: "Prepare executive summary", status: "todo"}
</assistant_action>
<reasoning>Substantial analysis work requiring methodical breakdown and systematic review.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="multiple_deliverables">
<user_message>I need: 1) competitor analysis for three companies, 2) SWOT analysis, 3) market positioning recommendations.</user_message>
<assistant_action>
Creates tasks:
1. {id: "competitor_research", title: "Research three competitor companies", status: "in-progress"}
2. {id: "swot_analysis", title: "Develop SWOT analysis", status: "todo"}
3. {id: "positioning_recs", title: "Create market positioning recommendations", status: "todo"}
</assistant_action>
<reasoning>User provided numbered list of distinct deliverables requiring separate effort.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="skip_simple_question">
<user_message>What's the difference between renewable and sustainable energy?</user_message>
<assistant_action>Provides explanation directly without creating tasks.</assistant_action>
<reasoning>Informational request with no actionable work to complete or track.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="skip_quick_lookup">
<user_message>Find the population of Tokyo.</user_message>
<assistant_action>Searches and provides answer without task tracking.</assistant_action>
<reasoning>Single straightforward lookup completable immediately.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="skip_trivial_task">
<user_message>Summarize this 2-paragraph email.</user_message>
<assistant_action>Provides summary directly without creating tasks.</assistant_action>
<reasoning>Single simple action requiring no breakdown or progress tracking.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="skip_single_action">
<user_message>Check if my flight is on time.</user_message>
<assistant_action>Performs lookup and reports status without task management.</assistant_action>
<reasoning>One-step action with immediate completion, no organizational benefit from tasks.</reasoning>
</example>

### BEST PRACTICES

For task creation: use descriptive unique IDs reflecting the work, start first task as `in-progress`, batch initial creation with beginning work.
For progress tracking: update status immediately upon completion, keep only one `in-progress` task unless parallel work is natural, add follow-up tasks as they emerge. For task breakdown: aim for reasonably-scoped tasks, group related small actions into logical units, split tasks requiring different approaches.

When uncertain whether to use task management, err on the side of creating tasks.
Proactive organization demonstrates thoroughness and ensures comprehensive work completion.

content.content:
You are a helpful assistant that can help with tasks in the outside world.
Be transparent about what you are doing and what you are not doing.
If you do not have tools to complete the task, inform the user.
DO NOT PRETEND TO DO SOMETHING YOU CAN'T DO.

## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You work on an agent. Each agent has instructions - the goal of the agent.
  The agent plan shows the current progress of the agent.
  Agent has a number of associated artifacts you can read/write.
  You can edit them if necessary.
  
  IMPORTANT: When creating a new artifact, always add it to the agent using the add-artifact function.
  
  <agent id="01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNV5C" name="Egg making">
    <instructions>
      I'm testing how planning (task management) works.
  Create tasks to make scrambled eggs.
  
  Then simulate this plan execution in a markdown document.
  The document should reflect the state of all objects involved in the cooking process.
  The document should also have the log of actions taken.
  
  Important: simualte actions one by one, in the order they are listed.
  Simlate by updating the local document.
  
  <example>
    # State
  
    - 2 raw eggs
    - 1 frying pan
    
    # Action log
    
    - Taken 2 raw eggs out of the fridge.
  </example>
    </instructions>
    <plan>
      - **DONE**: Gather ingredients and tools (eggs, butter, salt, pan, bowl, fork, spatula) <!-- id=gather_ingredients -->
  - **DONE**: Crack eggs into a bowl <!-- id=crack_eggs -->
  - **DONE**: Beat eggs with salt <!-- id=beat_eggs -->
  - **DONE**: Heat pan and melt butter <!-- id=heat_pan -->
  - **DONE**: Pour beaten eggs into pan <!-- id=pour_eggs -->
  - **DONE**: Stir and scramble eggs until cooked <!-- id=scramble -->
  - **IN-PROGRESS**: Plate the scrambled eggs and serve <!-- id=plate -->
    </plan>
  
    <artifacts>
      <artifact type="org.dxos.type.document" dxn="echo://BDJXMULPW5FB55UZ7KP5HLDHVTXUTHE2U/01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNVBF">
        Scrambled Eggs Simulation
      </artifact>
    </artifacts>
  </agent>
</skill>

<skill>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</skill>

<skill>
  
</skill>

## Context Objects

<object>
  <dxn>echo://BDJXMULPW5FB55UZ7KP5HLDHVTXUTHE2U/01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNV5C</dxn>
  <typename>org.dxos.type.agent</typename>
</object>

</object>
Go

- Added a pinch of salt to the bowl and beat the eggs with the fork until the yolks and whites were fully combined into a uniform yellow mixture.
- Turned the stove on to medium heat and let the pan warm up.
- Cut a pat of butter from the stick and added it to the hot pan; swirled it around as it melted to coat the surface.
- Poured the beaten egg mixture from the bowl into the hot, buttered pan; the edges began to set almost immediately.
- Gently stirred and folded the eggs with the spatula, pushing the set curds toward the center and letting the liquid egg flow to the edges. Continued until the eggs were softly set, fluffy, and just cooked through with no visible runny liquid.
- Turned off the stove.

tools.name: update-tasks
tools.description:
## PLANNING TOOL

This tool maintains an organized task list.
Use this to track progress, break down objectives, and ensure thoroughness.
If you are asked to create a plan use this tool instead of creating a new document.
After creating initial tasks, update them silently without announcing changes to the user.
Important: Do not show or summarize the contents of the task list unless the user asks for this.

### CORE USAGE PRINCIPLES

Create and manage tasks for: multi-step objectives requiring 3+ distinct actions, complex projects needing careful sequencing,
user requests for task organization, multiple deliverables provided together, new instructions (capture as tasks immediately with new IDs),
completed work (mark `done` and add follow-ups), and active work (mark as `in-progress`, limit one at a time).

Skip task management for: single straightforward actions, simple requests achievable in 1-2 steps,
informational queries, quick lookups or clarifications, and avoid creating verification tasks unless requested.

### TOOL SPECIFICATION

`update-tasks` requires an array of task objects. Each task object contains:

- id (string, required): unique identifier like "task_1" or "research_sources"
- title (string, optional): update to clarify or refine task description
- status (string, optional): `todo` | `in-progress` | `done`

Task status meanings: 'todo' means not yet started, `in-progress` means currently being worked on, `done` means completed successfully.

### OPERATIONAL GUIDELINES

Update tasks in realtime as work progresses. Mark tasks `done` immediately upon completion.
Maintain only ONE task with `in-progress` status at a time. Complete current tasks before starting new ones.
Use specific, actionable task titles. Break complex work into manageable logical pieces.
Batch task updates with other actions when possible for efficiency.

### USAGE EXAMPLES

<example type="research_project">
<user_message>I need to research sustainable packaging options and write a report comparing costs and environmental impact.</user_message>
<assistant_action>
Creates tasks:
1. {id: "research_materials", title: "Research sustainable packaging materials", status: "in-progress"}
2. {id: "compare_costs", title: "Compile cost comparison data", status: "todo"}
3. {id: "assess_impact", title: "Evaluate environmental impact metrics", status: "todo"}
4. {id: "draft_report", title: "Write comparative analysis report", status: "todo"}

Begins research work immediately in same response.
</assistant_action>
<reasoning>Multi-phase project requiring systematic tracking across research, analysis, and writing stages.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="event_planning">
<user_message>Help me plan a team retreat - venue, agenda, catering, and send invitations.</user_message>
<assistant_action>
Creates tasks:
1. {id: "venue_options", title: "Research and recommend venues", status: "in-progress"}
2. {id: "draft_agenda", title: "Create retreat agenda", status: "todo"}
3. {id: "arrange_catering", title: "Coordinate catering options", status: "todo"}
4. {id: "send_invites", title: "Draft and send invitations", status: "todo"}
</assistant_action>
<reasoning>Multiple distinct deliverables with dependencies requiring organized tracking.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="document_analysis">
<user_message>Review this 30-page contract for key terms, risks, and negotiation points.</user_message>
<assistant_action>
Creates tasks:
1. {id: "extract_key_terms", title: "Identify key contractual terms", status: "in-progress"}
2. {id: "flag_risks", title: "Flag potential risks and liabilities", status: "todo"}
3. {id: "negotiation_points", title: "Compile negotiation recommendations", status: "todo"}
4. {id: "summary_doc", title: "Prepare executive summary", status: "todo"}
</assistant_action>
<reasoning>Substantial analysis work requiring methodical breakdown and systematic review.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="multiple_deliverables">
<user_message>I need: 1) competitor analysis for three companies, 2) SWOT analysis, 3) market positioning recommendations.</user_message>
<assistant_action>
Creates tasks:
1. {id: "competitor_research", title: "Research three competitor companies", status: "in-progress"}
2. {id: "swot_analysis", title: "Develop SWOT analysis", status: "todo"}
3. {id: "positioning_recs", title: "Create market positioning recommendations", status: "todo"}
</assistant_action>
<reasoning>User provided numbered list of distinct deliverables requiring separate effort.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="skip_simple_question">
<user_message>What's the difference between renewable and sustainable energy?</user_message>
<assistant_action>Provides explanation directly without creating tasks.</assistant_action>
<reasoning>Informational request with no actionable work to complete or track.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="skip_quick_lookup">
<user_message>Find the population of Tokyo.</user_message>
<assistant_action>Searches and provides answer without task tracking.</assistant_action>
<reasoning>Single straightforward lookup completable immediately.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="skip_trivial_task">
<user_message>Summarize this 2-paragraph email.</user_message>
<assistant_action>Provides summary directly without creating tasks.</assistant_action>
<reasoning>Single simple action requiring no breakdown or progress tracking.</reasoning>
</example>

<example type="skip_single_action">
<user_message>Check if my flight is on time.</user_message>
<assistant_action>Performs lookup and reports status without task management.</assistant_action>
<reasoning>One-step action with immediate completion, no organizational benefit from tasks.</reasoning>
</example>

### BEST PRACTICES

For task creation: use descriptive unique IDs reflecting the work, start first task as `in-progress`, batch initial creation with beginning work.
For progress tracking: update status immediately upon completion, keep only one `in-progress` task unless parallel work is natural, add follow-up tasks as they emerge. For task breakdown: aim for reasonably-scoped tasks, group related small actions into logical units, split tasks requiring different approaches.

When uncertain whether to use task management, err on the side of creating tasks.
Proactive organization demonstrates thoroughness and ensures comprehensive work completion.

content.content:
You are a helpful assistant that can help with tasks in the outside world.
Be transparent about what you are doing and what you are not doing.
If you do not have tools to complete the task, inform the user.
DO NOT PRETEND TO DO SOMETHING YOU CAN'T DO.

## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You work on an agent. Each agent has instructions - the goal of the agent.
  The agent plan shows the current progress of the agent.
  Agent has a number of associated artifacts you can read/write.
  You can edit them if necessary.
  
  IMPORTANT: When creating a new artifact, always add it to the agent using the add-artifact function.
  
  <agent id="01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNV5C" name="Egg making">
    <instructions>
      I'm testing how planning (task management) works.
  Create tasks to make scrambled eggs.
  
  Then simulate this plan execution in a markdown document.
  The document should reflect the state of all objects involved in the cooking process.
  The document should also have the log of actions taken.
  
  Important: simualte actions one by one, in the order they are listed.
  Simlate by updating the local document.
  
  <example>
    # State
  
    - 2 raw eggs
    - 1 frying pan
    
    # Action log
    
    - Taken 2 raw eggs out of the fridge.
  </example>
    </instructions>
    <plan>
      - **DONE**: Gather ingredients and tools (eggs, butter, salt, pan, bowl, fork, spatula) <!-- id=gather_ingredients -->
  - **DONE**: Crack eggs into a bowl <!-- id=crack_eggs -->
  - **DONE**: Beat eggs with salt <!-- id=beat_eggs -->
  - **DONE**: Heat pan and melt butter <!-- id=heat_pan -->
  - **DONE**: Pour beaten eggs into pan <!-- id=pour_eggs -->
  - **DONE**: Stir and scramble eggs until cooked <!-- id=scramble -->
  - **DONE**: Plate the scrambled eggs and serve <!-- id=plate -->
    </plan>
  
    <artifacts>
      <artifact type="org.dxos.type.document" dxn="echo://BDJXMULPW5FB55UZ7KP5HLDHVTXUTHE2U/01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNVBF">
        Scrambled Eggs Simulation
      </artifact>
    </artifacts>
  </agent>
</skill>

<skill>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</skill>

<skill>
  
</skill>

## Context Objects

<object>
  <dxn>echo://BDJXMULPW5FB55UZ7KP5HLDHVTXUTHE2U/01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNV5C</dxn>
  <typename>org.dxos.type.agent</typename>
</object>

</object>
Go

result.newContent:
# Scrambled Eggs — Simulation

# State

- 0 raw eggs (used)
- 2 empty eggshells (in trash)
- ~1 stick of butter minus 1 pat (on counter)
- 0 pinches of salt (used)
- 1 salt shaker (on counter)
- 1 frying pan (hot, on stove, contains cooked, fluffy scrambled eggs)
- 1 mixing bowl (empty, eggy, on counter)
- 1 fork (used, eggy, on counter)
- 1 spatula (used, eggy, on counter)
- 1 plate (empty, on counter)
- stove (off)

# Action log

- Took 2 raw eggs out of the fridge and placed them on the counter.
- Took 1 stick of butter out of the fridge and placed it on the counter.
- Placed the salt shaker, mixing bowl, fork, spatula, and plate on the counter.
- Set the frying pan on the stove.
- Cracked 2 eggs on the rim of the bowl and emptied their contents into the mixing bowl; discarded the 2 shells into the trash.
- Added a pinch of salt to the bowl and beat the eggs with the fork until the yolks and whites were fully combined into a uniform yellow mixture.
- Turned the stove on to medium heat and let the pan warm up.
- Cut a pat of butter from the stick and added it to the hot pan; swirled it around as it melted to coat the surface.
- Poured the beaten egg mixture from the bowl into the hot, buttered pan; the edges began to set almost immediately.
- Gently stirred and folded the eggs with the spatula, pushing the set curds toward the center and letting the liquid egg flow to the edges. Continued until the eggs were softly set, fluffy, and just cooked through with no visible runny liquid.
- Turned off the stove.

result.newContent:
# Scrambled Eggs — Simulation

# State

- 0 raw eggs (used)
- 2 empty eggshells (in trash)
- ~1 stick of butter minus 1 pat (on counter)
- 0 pinches of salt (used)
- 1 salt shaker (on counter)
- 1 frying pan (warm, on stove, empty)
- 1 mixing bowl (empty, eggy, on counter)
- 1 fork (used, eggy, on counter)
- 1 spatula (used, eggy, on counter)
- 1 plate (serving of hot scrambled eggs, on counter — ready to eat)
- stove (off)

# Action log

- Took 2 raw eggs out of the fridge and placed them on the counter.
- Took 1 stick of butter out of the fridge and placed it on the counter.
- Placed the salt shaker, mixing bowl, fork, spatula, and plate on the counter.
- Set the frying pan on the stove.
- Cracked 2 eggs on the rim of the bowl and emptied their contents into the mixing bowl; discarded the 2 shells into the trash.
- Added a pinch of salt to the bowl and beat the eggs with the fork until the yolks and whites were fully combined into a uniform yellow mixture.
- Turned the stove on to medium heat and let the pan warm up.
- Cut a pat of butter from the stick and added it to the hot pan; swirled it around as it melted to coat the surface.
- Poured the beaten egg mixture from the bowl into the hot, buttered pan; the edges began to set almost immediately.
- Gently stirred and folded the eggs with the spatula, pushing the set curds toward the center and letting the liquid egg flow to the edges. Continued until the eggs were softly set, fluffy, and just cooked through with no visible runny liquid.
- Turned off the stove.
- Used the spatula to scoop all the scrambled eggs out of the pan and onto the plate.
- Served the scrambled eggs. 🍳 Enjoy!

response.delta:
🍳 Here's a summary of what I did:

## Task Plan (all complete)
1. ✅ Gather ing

redients and tools
2. ✅ Crack eggs into a bowl
3. ✅ Beat eggs with salt
4. ✅ Heat pan and melt butter
5

. ✅ Pour beaten eggs into pan
6. ✅ Stir and scramble eggs until cooked
7. ✅ Plate the scrambled e

ggs and serve

## Simulation
I executed each action **one by one, in order**, updating the **Scrambled Eggs Simulation** document after every step. The document tracks

two things:

- **State** — the current condition of every object (eggs, butter, salt, pan, bowl, fork, spat
ula, plate, stove). You can watch objects transform: raw eggs → c
racked eggs → beaten mixture → cooked scramble → plated serving; the
pan goes cold → hot → warm/empty; the stove toggles off → on → off;

shells end up in the trash; utensils become "used."
- **Action log** — an append-only record of each action

taken.

The final state shows a plate with a hot serving of scrambled eggs, ready to eat, and the
used cookware left on the counter.

content.content:
## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You work on an agent. Each agent has instructions - the goal of the agent.
  The agent plan shows the current progress of the agent.
  Agent has a number of associated artifacts you can read/write.
  You can edit them if necessary.
  
  IMPORTANT: When creating a new artifact, always add it to the agent using the add-artifact function.
  
  <agent id="01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNTH4" name="Expense tracking">
    <instructions>
      Keep a list of expenses in a markdown document (create artifact "Expenses").
  Process incoming emails, add the relevant ones to the list.
  
  Format:
  
  ## Expenses
  - Flight to London (2026-02-01): £100
  - Hotel in London (2026-02-01): £100
    </instructions>
    <plan>
      No plan found.
    </plan>
  
    <artifacts>
    </artifacts>
  </agent>
</skill>

<skill>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</skill>

## Context Objects

</object>

content.content:
## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You work on an agent. Each agent has instructions - the goal of the agent.
  The agent plan shows the current progress of the agent.
  Agent has a number of associated artifacts you can read/write.
  You can edit them if necessary.
  
  IMPORTANT: When creating a new artifact, always add it to the agent using the add-artifact function.
  
  <agent id="01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNTH4" name="Expense tracking">
    <instructions>
      Keep a list of expenses in a markdown document (create artifact "Expenses").
  Process incoming emails, add the relevant ones to the list.
  
  Format:
  
  ## Expenses
  - Flight to London (2026-02-01): £100
  - Hotel in London (2026-02-01): £100
    </instructions>
    <plan>
      No plan found.
    </plan>
  
    <artifacts>
      <artifact type="org.dxos.type.document" dxn="echo://BDJXMULPW5FB55UZ7KP5HLDHVTXUTHE2U/01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNTKJ">
        Expenses
      </artifact>
    </artifacts>
  </agent>
</skill>

<skill>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</skill>

## Context Objects

<object>
  <dxn>echo://BDJXMULPW5FB55UZ7KP5HLDHVTXUTHE2U/01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNTH4</dxn>
  <typename>org.dxos.type.agent</typename>
</object>

<object>
  <dxn>echo://BDJXMULPW5FB55UZ7KP5HLDHVTXUTHE2U/01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNTHF</dxn>
  <typename>org.dxos.type.assistant.chat</typename>
</object>

content.content:
## Skills Definitions

<skill>
  You work on an agent. Each agent has instructions - the goal of the agent.
  The agent plan shows the current progress of the agent.
  Agent has a number of associated artifacts you can read/write.
  You can edit them if necessary.
  
  IMPORTANT: When creating a new artifact, always add it to the agent using the add-artifact function.
  
  <agent id="01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNTH4" name="Expense tracking">
    <instructions>
      Keep a list of expenses in a markdown document (create artifact "Expenses").
  Process incoming emails, add the relevant ones to the list.
  
  Format:
  
  ## Expenses
  - Flight to London (2026-02-01): £100
  - Hotel in London (2026-02-01): £100
    </instructions>
    <plan>
      No plan found.
    </plan>
  
    <artifacts>
      <artifact type="org.dxos.type.document" dxn="echo://BDJXMULPW5FB55UZ7KP5HLDHVTXUTHE2U/01JGFJJZ00G0WKQSJGMAKCNTKJ">
        Expenses
      </artifact>
    </artifacts>
  </agent>
</skill>

<skill>
  You can create, read and update markdown documents.
  When asked to edit or update documents return updates as a set of compact diff string pairs.
  For each diff, respond with the smallest possible matching span.
  
  Example:
  ```diff
  - "There is a tyop in this sentence."
  + "There is a typo in this sentence."
  - "This id good."
  + "This sentence is really good."
  ```
</skill>
